sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: on the plus side: can help meals feel more complete and usable because fats sometimes improve the whole plate, not just their own stats. efficient energy source because that can be a strength in the right context. small amounts can change a whole meal because that leverage matters when the fat quality is good.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: now the downsides: easy to overpour without noticing because practical portion creep is real. easy to overuse without noticing because liquid calories are slippery in the kitchen. calorie density is brutally high because tiny serving errors matter here.

closing.summary: Lard does have some solid upside. but the tradeoffs keep it from climbing higher. best in controlled amounts where fat quality is the main reason to use it.
closing.useCaseNote: Best in controlled amounts where fat quality is the main reason to use it.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?
